title: Ebola scare in Bengaluru: Ugandan woman with 'signs' isolated at airport
content: A 28-year-old Ugandan woman arriving in Bengaluru has been isolated for Ebola testing as a precaution. While not exhibiting fever, airport health officials observed fatigue, prompting sample collection. Samples have been sent to the National Institute of Virology in Pune for analysis.

title: Byju’s founder Raveendran sentenced to 6 months in jail for contempt of court: Report
content: The ruling marks another setback for the entrepreneur, who is already facing legal challenges from overseas investors across multiple jurisdictions. In the United States, lenders are attempting to recover losses tied to a soured $1.2 billion loan.

title: ‘If you can’t give us roads, give us choppers’: Chhattisgarh villagers to Amit Shah
content: Residents of a secluded Chhattisgarh village, feeling overlooked after a decade of an unfinished road project, have taken a bold step in their appeal to the Union home minister: if roads are not feasible, then they request a helicopter to handle urgent situations.

title: Arun Dhumal Exclusive: Sooryavanshi embodies IPL's tagline of 'where talent meets opportunity'
content: In an IPL season bereft of the talent and regular presence of MS Dhoni and Rohit Sharma, 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has seemingly taken over the mantle. As the teenager dazzles with explosive batting, IPL Chairman Arun Dhumal says the BCCI and Rajasthan Royals must help nurture his rise and prepare him for the challenges ahead.

title: Cloudflare lays off 1,100 employees; CEO says company isn’t struggling
content: Cloudflare is laying off over 1,100 employees, about 20% of its workforce, to restructure around AI adoption. CEO Matthew Prince explained the cuts target 'measurers' – roles like middle management and operations – as AI enhances efficiency. He believes AI will boost productivity for 'builders' and 'sellers', allowing greater investment in growth areas.

title: Bank holiday today: Are banks open or closed for Bakri Eid? Check state-wise list
content: Banks across numerous Indian states are closed today for Eid-ul-Adha, impacting branch services like cheque clearance and cash deposits. Customers are urged to verify local holiday schedules. While physical banking is halted, digital services including internet banking, mobile apps, and ATMs remain fully operational. Plan ahead for essential branch tasks and utilize online channels to avoid disruption.

title: India rips into Pakistan at UN, flags 'bleeding India by a thousand cuts' doctrine
content: India strongly criticized Pakistan at the UN Security Council, accusing it of sponsoring cross-border terrorism and violating sovereignty through decades of aggression. India's envoy stated Pakistan's actions, including its "doctrine of bleeding India by a thousand cuts," expose its hollow rhetoric on the UN Charter.

title: 'We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system': Jeff Bezos to Amazon users
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Govt panel to examine 'unnatural demographic changes' due to illegal immigration
content: A high-level committee, headed by retired Supreme Court judge Prakash Prabhakar Naolekar, has been formed to examine demographic changes in India, including those caused by illegal immigration and abnormal population shifts. The committee will assess causes and impacts, recommending policy and legal frameworks to address these challenges within a year.

title: Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: Norway Chess: Divya beats India No. 1 Humpy; no joy for Gukesh, Pragg
content: Youngster Divya Deshmukh continued her fine start at Norway Chess by defeating country No. 1 Koneru Humpy in an Armageddon tiebreak after a tactical classical draw. It was a bleak day for India in the open section, however, as R Praggnanandhaa lost in classical to Alireza Firouzja, while D Gukesh fell to Wesley So in Armageddon.

title: Megan Thee Stallion recalls mother’s brain tumour battle amid Klay split
content: Megan Thee Stallion and the four time NBA champion Klay Thompson had a pretty brutal end to their relationship after she publicly accused him of infidelity and being commitment phobic towards her. While Klay Thompson has remained silent about all the allegations, many have remained concerned about Megan Thee Stallion, considering her history with depression. Amid all the mess, Megan Thee Stallion’s words about losing her mother have resurfaced.

title: Chiefs linked to ex-Patriots WR amid Rashee Rice legal uncertainty
content: The former New England Patriots’ wide receiver, Stefon Diggs, has remained an unrestricted free agent since the team released him in March after a dominating performance. Amid this, the star player has now been linked to the Kansas City Chiefs for the upcoming season, considering the messy situation involving Rashee Rice as Rice continues to be in jail for the next month.

title: Amanda Nunes and Nina Nunes’ 2026 net worth and lifestyle
content: MMA power couple Amanda and Nina Nunes boast an estimated combined net worth of $5-7 million by 2026. Amanda's historic dual-division championship reign and lucrative UFC career, coupled with Nina's steady run and coaching, fueled their financial success.  Their investments and private lifestyle underscore a remarkable journey from combat sports to respected family life.

title: Fact check: Is Shai Gilgeous-Alexander sidelined for Game 5 vs Spurs?
content: Social media claims of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander being out for Game 5 with an oblique injury are false; he is healthy and expected to start. The rumor likely stems from an old abdominal strain. The real injury concerns for OKC are Jalen Williams (questionable, hamstring) and Ousmane Dieng (out, soleus), impacting their Game 5 prospects.

title: Rajat Patidar walks the talk with a six-hitting masterclass in Dharamsala
content: Rajat Patidar backed his pre-match claim of preferring pace over spin with a breathtaking 93 off 33 balls against Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. The RCB skipper smashed nine sixes -- seven against pacers -- and now has 41 sixes this IPL season, with 27 coming against fast bowlers. Batting at No. 4, Patidar has scored 486 runs in 14 matches at a strike rate of 196.76, underlining his impact as one of RCB's most destructive middle-order batters.

title: Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for May 27 - check list
content: Stock market recommendations: AIA Engineering, State Bank of India (SBI), Hindustan Copper are the top stocks to buy on May 27, 2026, according to Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares.

title: 'Architect of October 7 massacre': Hamas military chief killed in Israel strike on Gaza
content: In a decisive military operation, Israel has taken out Muhammad Odeh, the newly appointed chief of Hamas's Al-Qassam Brigades, who played a crucial role in the October 7 assaults. This action is part of a broader campaign targeting Hamas leadership figures like Yahya Sinwar and Ismail Haniyeh.

title: AI and You: AI vs UPSC—three chatbots attempt India’s toughest exam
content: A comparative test of leading AI models on actual UPSC Prelims papers reveal how closely modern systems can mirror human-level preparation, handling history and polity well but struggling with precise current affairs and technical distinctions that often decide exam outcomes.

title: 'More than problematic': US Senator on Pakistan's role in Iran peace talks
content: The remarks came after Pakistan's defence minister Khawaja Muhammad Asif rejected US President Donald Trump’s appeal for Islamabad to join the Abraham Accords. Asif said Pakistan would not back any agreement that went against the country’s "fundamental ideologies". The Abraham Accords were brokered by the United States in 2020 and led to the normalisation of diplomatic, economic and security relations between Israel and several Arab nations.

title: Happy Eid: 70 Eid Mubarak wishes, messages and quotes to share on Bakrid
content: Navigating the flurry of Eid-ul-Adha greetings can be overwhelming. This guide offers 60 ready-to-use messages, from heartfelt family wishes and foodie friend prompts to professional greetings and quick replies.

title: With bizarre dismissal, Sai scripts unwanted IPL record, becomes first to...
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru stormed into the IPL 2026 final after thrashing Gujarat Titans by 92 runs. Rajat Patidar starred with an unbeaten 93 off 33 balls as RCB posted 254/5. Gujarat collapsed during the chase, with Sai Sudharsan creating an unwanted IPL record after a bizarre hit-wicket dismissal. Rahul Tewatia’s 68 was not enough as GT were bowled out for 162.

title: Revisiting Kargil: The story of how IAF thrashed Pakistan in Op Safed Sagar
content: In summer 1999, the Indian Air Force launched Operation Safed Sagar, a critical air-to-ground campaign to dislodge Pakistani intruders from Kargil heights. Facing unprecedented high-altitude challenges and early setbacks, the IAF rapidly adapted. By integrating advanced targeting systems like the Litening pod, they achieved precision strikes, notably at Tiger Hill, turning the tide and showcasing air power's decisive role.

title: Government softens stance on repossession of Delhi Gymkhana Club premises
content: The Centre clarified in Delhi High Court that June 5 was an "option" for the Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate, not a forced handover. This statement offered relief to club members and employees after a previous letter indicated a forceful repossession for defence purposes. The government's revised stance suggests a more restrained approach to the club's eviction.

title: Patidar stands alongside Dhoni and Rohit in historic IPL captaincy record
content: Rajat Patidar's explosive 93* powered Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a dominant 92-run victory over Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1, securing their spot in a second consecutive IPL final. This remarkable win places RCB among elite franchises and Patidar in a select group of captains achieving this feat.

title: ED raids former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s houses in CMRL pay-off case
content: ED searches unfolded at 12 locations across Kerala, including residence of former Minister and T. Veena’s husband, P.A. Mohamed Riyas, in Kozhikode, and houses of mining company’s promoters, among other localities

title: The judiciary’s role in complete justice
content: The Supreme Court is empowered under Article 142 to pass necessary orders to deliver complete justice where existing laws fail to provide a specific remedy. While this power acts as a ‘constitutional safety valve’, critics argue that it may lead to judicial encroachment upon the domain of the Executive or the Legislature

title: India home to 191 species of cockroaches, 60% of them are endemic, says ZSI study
content: Scientists from Zoological Survey of India and Prof Ramkrishna More College, have developed the first and largest DNA barcode reference library for the cockroaches of peninsular India

title: WB government directs districts to set up holding centres for detained illegal foreigners
content: The development comes days after Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari said that his government would follow a new policy of “detect, delete and deport” while dealing with “illegal infiltrators”. Last year in May, Union Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) directed all States and Union Territories to identify and verify illegal immigrants from Bangladesh and Myanmar (Rohingyas) within 30 days

title: What Tamil Nadu CM Vijay really needs to see at Fort St. George
content: A closer look beyond the museum and St. Mary’s Church shows Fort St. George struggling under poor maintenance, fragmented ownership, and years of official neglect, raising urgent questions about how Tamil Nadu intends to protect the historic seat of its own government

title: Punjab civic polls: 61.5% voter turnout amid scuffles
content: Polling through ballot papers began at 8 a.m. and will continue till 5 p.m; the counting of votes will take place on May 29

title: Suspected Ebola case triggers health alert in Bengaluru
content: Bengaluru District Surgeon and Medical Superintendent of the hospital, Anil Kumar Banagar, said the woman, suspected to have developed symptoms, had recently travelled from Uganda to Ahmedabad and had later arrived in Bengaluru

title: Power outage on Girivalam path in Tiruvannamalai raises safety concern
content: Tangedco officials said that power outages on the path occur mainly due to strong winds, these problems are attended to once the weather conditions are back to normal

title: Fuel shortage hits pumps amid panic buying; OMCs call for calm, say supply is steady
content: Petrol pump owners report dwindling supplies and long queues as demand peaks due to farming activity; some impose curbs on sale fearing supply disruption; OMCs assure public of “normal operations with steady fuel availability”, advise against hoarding

title: Chhattisgarh Congress president announces indefinite stir after party leader booked during poll campaigning
content: The Opposition party alleges that the Arms Act was slapped to influence the upcoming Shivnandanpur Nagar Panchayat elections

title: Farmers criticise cattle slaughter policy as buyers skip weekly market in Hassan
content: Hassan district unit of the Karnataka Prantha Raitha Sangha stage protest demanding withdrawal of law that prohibits cattle slaughter

title: Raichur MP raises concern over proposed amendments to Information Technology Act
content: Citing the proposed expansion of the scope of Part III of the rules, Mr. Naik said that the move appears to extend ‘a publisher-oriented framework to user-generated news and current affairs content on the Internet’

title: Supreme Court to pronounce verdict on Bihar SIR tomorrow
content: Petitions had accused ECI of arbitrarily assuming powers to “determine citizenship” while overriding limitations clearly prescribed in parliamentary laws, rules and its own manual without providing ‘any good reason’

title: Pradhan discusses CBSE payment gateway overhaul with four public sector banks
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan emphasises need for a robust, reliable, and student-friendly payment ecosystem, particularly for post-examination services such as re-evaluation, obtaining photocopies of answer sheets, and other fee-based processes.

title: NGO says Constitution tells citizens to protect environment, asks Chief Justice of India Surya Kant to withdraw remarks against environmentalists
content: They pointed out that the Constitution of India recognises the importance of environmental protection

title: Haryana lawyer moves Election Commission to register Cockroach Janta Party
content: While the registration of a party is optional, it enables a political outfit to claim certain benefits under law, such as accepting donations

title: When a DMK MLA was arrested for conducting an inspection and a journalist landed behind bars
content: While elected representatives are empowered to inspect government offices within their constituencies, no such authority extends to party functionaries

title: K9 warriors and robotic dogs steal spotlight at Exercise PRAGATI 2026
content: Indian Army showcases blend of canine combat skills and future warfare technology at multilateral military exercise in Meghalaya

title: Murder of siblings and a police encounter that rocked Coimbatore 16 years ago
content: The Supreme Court, on August 1, 2019, confirmed the death sentence of Manoharan, finding the case to be of the “rarest of rare category”

title: PM reaffirms stability, prosperity in Indo-Pacific in meetings with Foreign Ministers of Australia, Japan
content: PM Modi emphasised Indo-Pacific stability in talks with Australia and Japan, highlighting defence and trade partnerships

title: Himachal panchayat polls: 78.53% polling in first phase, women voters outnumber men
content: Shimla recorded 79.80% voter turnout, Mandi 79.39%, Una 78.76%, Bilaspur 77.39%, Kangra 75.75%, Hamirpur 73.05% and Kinnaur 73.59%

title: Mundamveli residents lay day-long siege to GCDA headquarters
content: Residents raise fresh concerns over the alleged violation of mandatory distance norms separating the 110 kV EHT line passing through their neighbourhood from the twin towers.

title: Rajya Sabha defections, constitutional questions
content: The recent episode involving the Aam Aadmi Party and the invocation of the provisions of the 10th Schedule highlights the need for judicial clarity

title: Jalagamparai and Bheeman falls in Jawadhu Hills open for tourists as water flow resumes due to rain
content: Tirupattur, Vellore, and Tiruvannamalai have been receiving rain, especially during night for the past few days

title: Two persons arrested for possession of ganja, narcotic tablets in Arakkonam
content: Investigation found the duo bought ganja from peddlers in Chittoor district in Andhra Pradesh whereas they placed an order for tablets with pharmacies in Mumbai

title: How safe is India’s critical national infrastructure?
content: Over the last few decades, critical infrastructure and services have been scaled up through automation, the Internet of Things and AI. However, this has also made them more vulnerable to attacks through IoT devices, highlighting the need for a policy framework to safeguard our infrastructure

title: Andhra Pradesh emerges as a pioneer in AI-driven healthcare innovations, says Minister
content: Satya Kumar Yadav promotes AI-driven diagnostic tests, says that these not only provide accurate results but also improve the speed of delivery of healthcare services and make tests affordable for people

title: CBSE refutes claim that its marking portal was compromised
content: The Board’s statement comes as an ethical hacker claims he had exposed the vulnerabilities of the portal and complained to CERT-In in February

title: Fast-track court frames charges against seven accused in Zubeen Garg death case
content: Four accused of murder based on SIT chargesheet; Garg died while swimming in Singapore in 2025; Assam CM claims he was murdered as part of a conspiracy; Singapore coroner ruled it an accidental drowning

title: Twisha Sharma death: Madhya Pradesh DGP defends Bhopal Police as CBI takes over case, launches probe
content: Kailash Makwana’s statement comes amid several allegations made by Twisha Sharma’s family such as institutional bias and procedural lapses by local police

title: India-U.S. and Quad frameworks on critical minerals take shape amid Chinese curbs
content: Agreement built upon continued engagements between two countries, including India becoming a signatory to the U.S.-led Pax Silica initiative on February 20, 2026.

title: Extreme heatwave hits A.P. as Vijayawada north soars to 47.3°C
content: The city felt like a furnace at 7 p.m. as well as the temperatures stood at 40°C, according to real-time weather information available on the Directorate of Economics and Statistics

title: Delhi High Court notice to Swati Chaturvedi on OpIndia’s plea against trial court order
content: The court said the material filed by OpIndia ‘prima facie nowhere shows’ that Chaturvedi was involved in ‘running extortion rackets’ as alleged

title: Andhra Pradesh launches initiative to clear water hyacinth and revive water bodies
content: MA&UD Principal Secretary says the unchecked growth of the weed has obstructed irrigation channels, degraded water quality, and disrupted aquatic ecosystems, posing challenges to agriculture and public health

title: Six killed and 21 injured as bus overturns on Lucknow-Agra Expressway
content: Eyewitness accounts indicate that the bus was traveling at a high speed when it collided with the divider and overturned

title: Quad FMs announce maritime, energy initiatives amidst Hormuz chokehold, tense South China Sea
content: FMs pledge collaboration on energy security, maritime surveillance, domain awareness; Quad joint statement calls for freedom of navigation, adherence to international law in Hormuz, Red Sea, South China Sea

title: Supreme Court to examine if law is diluting India’s wetland count
content: The Convention expressly included both natural and artificial wetlands, whether permanent or temporary, without distinction, senior advocate Gopal Sankaranarayanan and advocate Anindita Mitra submitted for the petitioners

title: New IIP series to track rare earth, PNG, water supply
content: Determine the monthly installment amount for a loan Estimate the returns on investments made through SIPs Find out maturity amount and interest earned on PPF Check maturity amount and interest earned on an FD Estimate the pension amount and corpus accumulated under NPS A Mutual Fund Calculator helps estimate the future value of investments

title: India plans anti-jamming upgrade for 258 Su-30MKI jets
content: Russia USA USA Russia France India N. Korea Russia China India India India India India India India India India India Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel Israel USA USA USA USA USA USA USA USA Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Iran Russia

title: ‘Maachh-bhaat’ to be served twice a week at Maa canteens at Rs 5 in Bengal
content: all 367 maa canteens across the state will serve fish twice a week . the meal will be available for just Rs 5 . a new menu is expected to be introduced soon, with the specific days for serving fish likely to be finalised .

title: 3 years on, acid attack survivor wins right to disability review
content: acid attack survivor, 28-year-old Reshma Qureshi, struggled for three years . she was assessed as "low vision" by a medical board last week . her disability ID card has now correctly certified her quantum of disability as 63% .

title: Trump, nearing 80, continues war on aging, sleep, and other enemies
content: american president Donald Trump turns 80 next month . he has spent years presenting himself as less an aging president than a human monument to stamina, fast food, and pure willpower . now, amid a war on Iran, global economic stress, and growing scrutiny about his age, he headed out to a presidential repair shop .

title: Being mute spectators no ground to prosecute in-laws for cruelty: SC
content: the in-laws of a woman cannot be prosecuted for cruelty or making dowry demands . such conduct is questionable on moral grounds, but does not automatically amount to criminal culpability . a bench of justices said that criminal law cannot be set in motion against every family member of the husband .

title: Anti-drone system on Pakistan border in 6 months: Amit Shah
content: home minister promises faster deployment of advanced surveillance systems, anti-drone technology and upgraded infrastructure along India’s western frontier . he inaugurates new barracks for women personnel of BSF during a visit to forward posts in Rajasthan bordering Pakistan .

title: Quad unveils new global initiatives after year of uncertainty: Key takeaways
content: the quad announced a series of initiatives in key areas like critical minerals, energy security and maritime surveillance in the Indian Ocean . it also announced plans to build a port in fiiji, hoping to supercharge the grouping’s “global good” agenda . the meeting condemned the attacks on commercial shipping vessels and opposed future measures inconsistent with UNCLOS .

title: Ex-Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan's House Raided By Probe Agency
content: It is alleged that a private company named Cochin Minerals And Rutile Ltd, made an illegal payment of Rs 1.72 crore to Vijayan's daughter T Veena's company Exalogic Solutions during 2018 to 2019.

title: Siddaramaiah Pushing For Multiple Deputy Chief Ministers, Will DK Shivakumar Agree?
content: Siddaramaiah camp pushes for a multiple deputy chief minister formula ahead of expected resignation, say sources

title: 'Illegal': India's Response To J&K References In China-Pak Joint Statement
content: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said that New Delhi "opposes and rejects" Islamabad's "illegal and forcible occupation of India's sovereign territory."

title: Opinion: Opinion | Degrees, SUVs, Gold: Why India's Most 'Qualified' Men Often Demand The Steepest Dowries
content: Education, ironically, is more of an enabler of the practice than a hindrance, per a 2023 study quoted by the BBC

title: 17-Year-Old Shot At Delhi Eatery After Objecting To Man Touching His Chair
content: The teenager was initially taken to Moolchand Hospital but was later referred to the AIIMS Trauma Centre, where he is undergoing treatment.

title: Pulwama Attack Mastermind Seen With AK-47 In Video Days Before Death
content: Hamza Burhan's name came up in connection with the February 2019 Pulwama attack in which 40 CRPF personnel were killed in a grenade attack on a convoy.

title: NPS vs Mutual Funds: Which One Can Make You Richer In Retirement?
content: If the goal is retirement security, and long-term investing, NPS has a structural advantage. If the goal is liquidity, mutual funds are more suitable.

title: Sexual Harassment Accused Gets Bail With One Year Ban On Social Media
content: Jodhpur/New Delhi, May 26 (IANS) The Rajasthan High Court has granted bail to a man accused of sexually harassing and stalking a minor girl, while imposing stringent conditions including a one-year ban on the use of all social media platforms such as

title: Israel Targets Hamas Military Chief, Who Led Intel During October 7 Attack
content: Mohammed Odeh served as head of Hamas intelligence during the October 7 massacre and was appointed approximately one week ago as successor to Ezzedine Al-Haddad

title: Weather Live Updates: Yellow Alert For Heat Wave In Delhi Today, Relief Likely On Friday
content: Weather Live Updates: The IMD has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in Delhi, forecasting a maximum temperature of 45 degrees Celsius.

title: US-Iran War LIVE: Iran Calls US Strikes 'Bad Faith', Warns Of Consequences As Talks Continue
content: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

title: Delhi Prepares "Power Master Plan" As Demand Surges In May Heatwave
content: The announcement comes as Delhi has already crossed 8,400 MW in peak power demand in May, close to last year's summer peak and significantly earlier than usual.

title: Ahead Of 80th Birthday, Trump Declares Himself In "Perfect" Health
content: Trump's doctor is expected to release a summary of the medical exam in coming days, although the amount of detail released is entirely at the White House's discretion.

title: 23-Year-Old Shot Dead In Delhi; Police Launch Probe
content: The forensic team gathered evidence from the crime scene. Multiple teams have been deployed to trace and arrest the accused involved in the crime, police added.

title: 3 Killed, 5 Injured As Speeding Truck Rams Devotees Near UP Temple: Cops
content: At least three people were killed and five others injured when a truck skidded off the road and overturned onto devotees gathered near a temple in Uttar Pradesh's Amroha district on Tuesday.

title: US Senator Calls Pakistan's Role In US-Iran Talks "Problematic"
content: Senator Lindsey Graham's remarks came as Pakistan's Defence Minister Khwaja Asif said he is not in favour of Islamabad joining the Abraham Accords

title: Cuba Appeals For International Help As US Intensifies Pressure
content: Donald Trump has openly mused about taking over communist Cuba, saying that after the US attack in January that ousted Nicolas Maduro in Venezuela, Havana could be his next target for military action and big change.

title: Dera chief Ram Rahim walks out on 30-day parole, his 16th since 2020
content: govt granted another 30 days' parole to Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh . he is serving 20 years in prison for raping two women disciples . his release was his 16th since 2020 and his second this year .

title: TMC crisis deepens: 6 MLAs and an MP turn up at Suvendu Adhikari's meet
content: 127 councillors from 11 civic bodies have quit so far . four-time MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar attended an administrative meeting of the CM in an apparent act of defiance . the turmoil continued later as two TMC MLAs held a closed-door "courtesy" meeting in the chamber of Rathindra Bose .

title: N Chandrasekaran unveils 3-year plan to cut losses in Tata new businesses
content: Reeba Zachariah is assistant corporate editor at The Times of India, Mumbai. She has been covering large Indian business houses such as the Tata Group. She also reports on a host of sectors like hospitality, retail, travel, liquor and consumer durables. She has been writing on mergers and  acquisitions and private equity. title: Hundreds try to flee to Bangladesh as Bengal CM Adhikari says quit India
content: dozens of Bangladeshis without the documents required to stay in India made a bee-line for the border exit in Bengal's North 24 Parganas on Tuesday . CM Suvendu Adhikari told all suspected infiltrators to leave or face action . the new BJP govt's "detect-delete-deport" strategy for illegal immigrants was implemented .

title: Three reports, one story: MiraOne launches precision medicine platform in Mumbai
content: a comprehensive blood test that promises to transform the way we study our body was launched on Saturday in Mumbai at ImagiNXT conference attended by an impressive audience . MiraOne generates three reports to tell one story from a single draw of blood . gives a complete picture - your blood markers, the DNA you inherited and how you respond to medicines .

title: Karnataka switch? Congress offers Rajya Sabha berth to CM Siddaramaiah
content: Manu Aiyappa has been with The Times of India for over two decades, currently serving as the Political Editor. His reporting focuses on politics, public policy, and in-depth features that aim to inform and engage a broad readership. Over the years, his work has been recognised by both the Press Club of Bangalore and the Karnataka Media Academy for its clarity, credibility, and relevance to public discourse. Read More

title: ED raids ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s house in CMRL-linked money laundering case
content: ED officials are carrying out raids at 10 locations linked to former chief minister . searches are also underway at the residence of CMRL Managing director Sasidharan Kartha and former minister Mohammed Riyas . the action follows the high court order permitting the ED to continue its investigation .
